skill.name: research-agent-optimization
skill.description: Optimize the research agent for rate limit handling, API call efficiency, web search integration fixes, and improved streaming UX with granular progress updates and source attribution.
skill.summary: Improve a research agent with better rate limits, search tools, streaming progress, and source presentation.
skill.author: BenderFendor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed file is Markdown guidance. The shell findings are inline code paths or examples, and no executable script, cryptographic primitive, or system reconnaissance command was found. A low warning remains because the skill asks the agent to use an external code-search tool for API verification.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited Markdown references as Ruby or shell execution. These lines contain project paths, file names, function names, command-line option text, or illustrative tool-call labels, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content appears inside SKILL.md as Markdown prose and inline code. There is no script file, code fence with shell execution, or instruction to execute these strings as commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe the skill, caching, streaming event names, frontend rendering, and invalid-query testing. No evidence found for weak cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, host enumeration, credential discovery, or similar risky behavior. The warning appears to be keyword-based noise from natural language text.

low_findings.title: External Tool Use Requires User Awareness
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to use exa-code to verify DuckDuckGo API patterns. This is not malicious, but it may cause the agent to rely on an external code-search service during implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and contextually related to API verification. It does not request secrets or unsafe execution, so the residual risk is operational rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Research agents can waste quota, hide progress, and return hard-to-use source data. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through practical backend and frontend improvements.
actual_capabilities: Defines backend changes for Gemini rate-limit handling and exponential backoff.
Specifies DuckDuckGo web and news search initialization checks.
Plans caching and batching for internal semantic news search.
Maps streaming tool events to clear user-facing progress messages.
Describes frontend source-card rendering instead of raw JSON blocks.
Lists verification checks for quota errors, search results, and source de-duplication.
limitations: It is implementation guidance, not a packaged library or runnable automation.
It assumes the target project uses the listed backend and frontend file paths.
It may need current DuckDuckGo API verification before implementation.
It does not include complete test code or migration scripts.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Agent Research Workflows
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to reduce duplicate calls, handle quota failures, and keep research sessions responsive.

use_cases.title: Improve Streaming Search Feedback
use_cases.description: Map tool activity into clear progress events so users see what the agent is doing during long searches.

use_cases.title: Present Research Sources Clearly
use_cases.description: Replace raw response data with readable source cards grouped by retrieval method.

prompt_templates.title: Review Current Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my research agent against this skill. Identify the missing rate-limit, search, streaming, and source-rendering changes.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Rate Limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the rate-limit handling plan. Add graceful 429 handling, exponential backoff, quota logging, and user-safe errors.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Search and Streaming
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the web search tools and streaming events so each tool call reports its name, query, result count, and duration.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Full Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the full optimization checklist, then verify caching, DuckDuckGo search, SSE progress, source cards, and error scenarios.

output_examples.input: A user asks why research results still show raw source data.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the response parsing and rendering paths, then recommends a Sources section with article cards and de-duplication.

output_examples.input: A developer reports frequent quota errors during article analysis.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes graceful 429 handling, shorter retry windows, quota warnings, and an optional mode that skips Gemini analysis.

output_examples.input: A product team wants clearer long-running search progress.
output_examples.output: The assistant maps each search and article-reading action to specific streaming messages with timestamps and durations.

best_practices: Verify the current DuckDuckGo package API before changing search tool initialization.
Keep user-facing error messages clear and avoid exposing stack traces.
Test repeated queries to confirm caching and source de-duplication work.
anti_patterns: Do not retry quota failures indefinitely or hide the cause from users.
Do not render raw JSON source blocks as the final research experience.
Do not add streaming messages that omit the active tool or query context.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance, target files, and verification checks for an existing research agent.

faq.question: Which model issue does it address?
faq.answer: It focuses on Gemini quota handling, faster model selection, and graceful fallback when limits are reached.

faq.question: Can it improve web search reliability?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides validation of DuckDuckGo initialization and fallback behavior when web search fails.

faq.question: Does it help frontend teams?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes source-card rendering, retry UI, and clearer progress display for streamed research.

faq.question: Is it tied to one codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The file paths and examples target a specific thesis project structure.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a low-risk note. The static alerts are Markdown false positives, but the skill mentions external code-search verification.
